hero.title: Les Rochers Blancs
hero.subtitle: Hotel & Restaurant at Semnoz
hero.tagline: A haven of peace at 1,700 meters altitude in the heart of Haute-Savoie mountains

summer.title: SUMMER Season
summer.closedDays: Closed on Mondays and Sunday Evenings

winter.title: WINTER Season
winter.hotelDates: From 21/12/26 to 7/1/27 and from 9/2/27 to 10/3/27 (+weekends January and March)
winter.closedDays: Closed on Mondays and Sunday Evenings

hotel.title: The Hotel
hotel.subtitle: 16 comfortable rooms at 1700m altitude
hotel.description: At Les Rochers Blancs, we offer you a haven of peace to rest after a busy day exploring the wonders of Semnoz. In a mountain setting at 1700 meters altitude, all our rooms are equipped with bathroom, WC, television and wifi.
hotel.description2: With our 16 comfortable and welcoming rooms, designed to offer you a relaxation space, with careful decoration and functional furniture. Enjoy a good night's sleep in our soft beds and appreciate the calm of the surrounding mountains.
hotel.groupNote: Possibility of booking outside opening periods for groups. Do not hesitate to contact us.
hotel.closingNote: Closing dates of Hotel Les Rochers Blancs differ from those of the Restaurant.

extras.title: Extras
extras.halfBoard: Half board: €88 minimum 3 days — price per day per person
extras.fullBoard: Full board: €112 minimum 3 days — price per day per person

restaurant.title: The Restaurant
restaurant.subtitle: Alpine gastronomy and local flavors
restaurant.description: Our chef elevates the products of the Savoyard terroir in a warm atmosphere with panoramic mountain views.
restaurant.groupDescription: We organize seminars over one or several days.

about.title: Our Story
about.subtitle: A haven of peace at the summit of Semnoz
about.description: Nestled at the summit of Semnoz, Les Rochers Blancs is an exceptional retreat where tradition and modernity meet. Our family has welcomed you for generations in this enchanting setting at 1700 meters altitude.

tradition.title: Tradition
tradition.description: Know-how passed down from generation to generation

nature.title: Nature
nature.description: In harmony with the alpine environment

bus.title: By Bus from Annecy
bus.description: 6 times a day in summer, weekends and during school holidays.

car.title: By Car
car.description: Highway A41 exit Annecy Sud. Follow direction Albertville to the center of Annecy. Follow the Semnoz (D41) for 17 km. Arrival at Crêt de Chatillon.

gps.title: GPS Access
gps.description: Maison du Semnoz, commune Viuz-la-Chiesaz. You arrive at the Semnoz station, the hotel is 2 km above.

partners.title: Our Partners
partners.description: Enhance your relaxation and escape moments during your stay at Semnoz with the professionals of our region.

All content on this site (texts, images, videos, logos, icons, sounds, software, layout, etc.) is protected by copyright and intellectual property law.

Any total or partial reproduction, representation, modification, publication, or adaptation of these elements, by any means whatsoever, is strictly prohibited without the prior written authorisation of Les Rochers Blancs.

Any unauthorised use shall be considered as infringement and prosecuted in accordance with applicable law.

items.answer: This site may contain hyperlinks to other websites. Les Rochers Blancs has no control over the content of these third-party sites and accepts no responsibility for their content or any damages resulting from their use.

items.question: Limitation of liability
items.answer:
The information contained on this site is as accurate as possible and is regularly updated. However, Les Rochers Blancs cannot guarantee the accuracy, completeness, or timeliness of the information provided.

The user therefore acknowledges using this information at their own risk. Les Rochers Blancs shall not be held liable for any direct or indirect damage resulting from accessing or using this site.

sections.title: Privacy policy
sections.intro: In accordance with the General Data Protection Regulation (GDPR — EU Regulation 2016/679) and the French Data Protection Act of 6 January 1978 (as amended), this policy describes how we collect and process your personal data.

items.question: Data controller
items.answer:
The data controller is:

Les Rochers Blancs
Address: Semnoz, 74000 Annecy, Haute-Savoie, France
Email: see Contact section

items.question: Data collected
items.answer:
We collect the following data:

• Identification data: surname, first name, email address, phone number
• Booking data: stay dates, room type, number of guests, special requests
• Browsing data: IP address, browser type, pages viewed, visit duration
• Contact data: messages sent via the contact form

items.question: Purposes of processing
items.answer:
Your data is collected for the following purposes:

• Managing accommodation and restaurant bookings
• Processing your enquiries and messages
• Sending communications related to your stay
• Improving our services and user experience
• Compliance with legal and regulatory obligations
• Invoicing and accounting management

items.question: Legal basis for processing
items.answer:
The processing of your data is based on:

• Performance of a contract (booking, stay)
• Your consent (contact form, cookies)
• Our legitimate interest (service improvement, security)
• Compliance with legal obligations (invoicing, accounting)

items.question: Data retention period
items.answer:
Your data is retained for the following periods:

• Customer and booking data: 3 years after last contact
• Invoicing data: 10 years (legal accounting obligation)
• Browsing data (cookies): 13 months maximum
• Contact messages: 3 years

Beyond these periods, your data is deleted or anonymised.

items.question: Data recipients
items.answer:
Your data may be shared with the following recipients:

• Our authorised staff (reception, restaurant, management)
• Our technical service providers (web hosting, maintenance)
• Competent authorities where required by law

Your data is never sold to third parties. No transfers outside the European Union are carried out.

items.question: Your rights
items.answer:
Under the GDPR, you have the following rights:

• Right of access: obtain confirmation that data concerning you is being processed
• Right of rectification: correct inaccurate or incomplete data
• Right to erasure: request deletion of your data
• Right to restriction: restrict the processing of your data
• Right to portability: receive your data in a structured format
• Right to object: object to the processing of your data

To exercise your rights, contact us by email (see Contact section). You may also lodge a complaint with the CNIL (www.cnil.fr).

items.question: Data security
items.answer:
We implement all appropriate technical and organisational measures to ensure the security and confidentiality of your personal data, including:

• Encryption of data in transit (HTTPS/TLS)
• Restricted access to personal data
• Regular and secure backups
• Regular security updates

sections.title: General terms & conditions
sections.intro: These general terms and conditions govern the use of this website and the accommodation and restaurant services offered by Les Rochers Blancs.

items.question: Purpose
items.answer: These general terms and conditions define the terms under which the Les Rochers Blancs website is made available, as well as the conditions for booking and staying at the establishment.

items.question: Bookings
items.answer:
Bookings can be made by phone, email, or via the website. Any booking is considered firm and final upon receipt of a deposit of 30% of the total stay amount.

A confirmation email is sent to the customer with the booking details (dates, room type, rate, cancellation conditions).

items.question: Rates
items.answer:
Rates are quoted in euros and include all taxes (TTC). They may be modified at any time. The applicable rate is the one in effect at the time of booking.

The tourist tax, set by the local authority, is added to the room price and is collected on behalf of the municipality.

items.question: Check-in and check-out
items.answer:
Rooms are available from 3:00 PM on the day of arrival. Check-out must be completed by 11:00 AM on the day of departure.

Early check-in or late check-out requests are subject to availability and may incur an additional charge.

items.question: Cancellation and modification
items.answer:
All cancellations must be notified in writing (email or letter).

• Cancellation more than 7 days before arrival: full refund of the deposit
• Cancellation between 7 and 3 days before arrival: 50% of the deposit retained
• Cancellation less than 3 days before arrival or no-show: full deposit retained

We recommend taking out cancellation insurance.

items.question: Liability
items.answer:
The establishment provides a safe for valuables. In accordance with applicable legislation, the hotelier's liability covers items deposited in the safe.

The establishment declines all responsibility for theft of items not deposited in the safe, as well as for vehicles parked in the car park.

items.question: House rules
items.answer:
Guests agree to comply with the establishment's house rules, in particular:

• Quiet hours between 10:00 PM and 7:00 AM
• No smoking throughout the establishment
• Pets are accepted under certain conditions (please enquire)
• Any damage to equipment or furniture will be charged

items.question: Applicable law and disputes
items.answer:
These general terms and conditions are governed by French law. In the event of a dispute, an amicable solution will be sought first.

In accordance with the provisions of the French Consumer Code, the customer may use the consumer mediation service free of charge. The competent mediator is: [To be completed].

Failing amicable resolution, the French courts shall have sole jurisdiction.

sections.title: Cookie policy
sections.intro: This site uses cookies and similar technologies to ensure its proper functioning and improve your browsing experience, in accordance with European Directive 2009/136/EC and CNIL recommendations.

items.question: What is a cookie?
items.answer: A cookie is a small text file stored on your device (computer, tablet, smartphone) when you visit a website. It allows the site to remember information about your browsing (language preferences, session identifiers, etc.).

items.question: Cookies used
items.answer:
We use the following categories of cookies:

• Strictly necessary cookies: essential for the site to function (session, authentication, language preferences). They cannot be disabled.

• Performance cookies: allow us to measure site traffic and analyse browsing to improve our services.

• Preference cookies: remember your choices (language, light/dark theme) to personalise your experience.

items.question: Retention period
items.answer: Cookies are stored for a maximum of 13 months in accordance with CNIL recommendations. Session cookies are deleted when the browser is closed.

items.question: Managing cookies
items.answer:
You can manage your cookie preferences at any time:

• Via your browser settings (Chrome, Firefox, Safari, Edge, etc.)
• By deleting cookies already stored

Disabling certain cookies may affect the site's operation and limit access to certain features.

For more information on managing cookies, visit the CNIL website: www.cnil.fr.

items.question: Third-party cookies
items.answer:
This site may embed content or services from third parties (Google Maps, videos, social networks). These third parties may set cookies governed by their own privacy policies.

We have no control over these third-party cookies. Please refer to the privacy policies of the relevant services for more information.
